eyebrow: Some rough edges are allowed to remain
headline: I won’t tidy what isn’t hurting you.
subheadline: Show me the untidy parts. I’ll tell you which create real cost and which deserve no more of your attention.
chatStarter: Rustam, the setup looks embarrassing, but most of it works. I need honesty about what actually deserves fixing.
proofLine: Rustam may recommend leaving harmless rough edges alone.
whyItWorks: The personal promise removes cleanup shame and makes restraint feel caring rather than dismissive.

eyebrow: Improve only disorder with an operating cost
headline: Separate harmless disorder from recurring operating cost.
subheadline: Map each rough edge by time lost, errors created, ownership friction, and maintenance burden; only costly disorder enters the improvement plan.
chatStarter: This setup is inconsistent. I need a defensible way to separate cosmetic disorder from work that repeatedly consumes time.
proofLine: Each improvement is tied to an observable operating cost.
whyItWorks: The precise classification protects effort while giving every proposed cleanup a professional burden of proof.

eyebrow: Good support should know when it has worked
headline: I want this month to make me unnecessary.
subheadline: We’ll name what you should handle more confidently, the evidence that proves it, and the point when continuing stops earning its place.
chatStarter: Rustam, I want help now and a clear sign that I can carry this without the same support later.
proofLine: Continuation is reviewed against capability, evidence, and remaining need.
whyItWorks: The candid personal aim makes independence emotional, memorable, and visibly opposed to manufactured dependence.

eyebrow: Ongoing guidance needs explicit completion criteria
headline: Set an exit condition for monthly support.
subheadline: Define the capability, measurable evidence, review date, and remaining risk that determine whether support continues, changes scope, or closes.
chatStarter: Monthly guidance needs a clear completion standard tied to stronger independent judgment, not continued activity.
proofLine: Continuation, scope change, and closure share explicit review criteria.
whyItWorks: The professional version turns an ethical intention into a reviewable operating rule for the engagement.
